scale.label: low
scale.definition: Essentially irrelevant to this group. The bill does not meaningfully touch their lives.

scale.label: moderate
scale.definition: First-order indirect effect only. The group is affected, but through a single step of consequence rather than being the direct subject of the bill.

scale.label: high
scale.definition: Direct and immediate effect. The group is an explicit subject of the bill and feels its effect firsthand.

values.label: Veteran
values.description: Has served in the U.S. armed forces, including reserves and National Guard with qualifying service.

values.label: Has a disability
values.description: Self-identified physical, mental, sensory, or cognitive disability, whether or not formally adjudicated.

values.label: Student
values.description: Currently enrolled in post-secondary education: undergraduate, graduate, or trade/vocational. K-12 legislation reaches users through parent_of_minor and the under_18 age bracket.

values.label: Has federal student loans
values.description: Holds or is repaying federal student loan debt, whether or not currently enrolled. Loan forgiveness, repayment, and interest legislation targets borrowers, most of whom are no longer students.

values.label: Parent of a minor child
values.description: Has at least one child under 18.

values.label: Caregiver of a dependent adult
values.description: Provides regular unpaid care to a dependent adult, such as an aging or disabled relative.

values.label: Other
values.description: Living arrangements outside owning or renting, e.g. living with family, unhoused, institutional housing.

values.label: Lawful permanent resident
values.description: Green-card holder; not a citizen.

values.label: Other immigration status
values.description: Visa holders, asylees, undocumented persons, and any status other than citizen or permanent resident.

values.label: Under 18
values.description: Minors. Users are adults, so this value is mainly a scoring target: bills aimed at children score here instead of smearing into a young-adult bracket.

values.label: Education
values.description: Teachers, faculty, school staff.

values.label: Healthcare
values.description: Clinical and allied health workers: doctors, nurses, aides, technicians, pharmacists.

values.label: Construction & trades
values.description: Skilled and manual trades; on-site building work.

values.label: Manufacturing & industrial
values.description: Factory and plant production work: machinists, assemblers, energy and mining.

values.label: Agriculture
values.description: Farming, ranching, fishing, forestry.

values.label: Transportation & logistics
values.description: Drivers, pilots, warehouse and supply-chain workers.

values.label: Public sector & first responders
values.description: Government employees, police, fire, EMS, military.

values.label: Professional & office
values.description: White-collar roles: finance, law, admin, management.

values.label: Retail & service
values.description: Retail, hospitality, food service, personal services.

values.label: Technology
values.description: Software, IT, hardware, and related roles.

values.label: Other
values.description: Occupations not captured above. Track selection rate; frequent use is a signal to add a bucket.

values.label: Employee
values.description: Works for an employer (W-2).

values.label: Self-employed / business owner
values.description: Self-employed, independent contractor, gig worker, or small-business owner.

values.label: Unemployed
values.description: Not working and seeking work.

values.label: Not in the labor force
values.description: Not working and not seeking work: homemaker, full-time student, unable to work.

values.label: LGBTQ+
values.description: Lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, and other sexual or gender minority identities. Covers gender identity as well as orientation; may be split into finer values later if bills differentiate.
